GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released the Draft National Water Policy 2025 for public consultation.
• The policy proposes a paradigm shift towards integrated river basin management, treating water as a shared resource rather than solely a state subject.
• Key recommendations include promoting water use efficiency in agriculture and industry, and adopting demand-side management strategies.
• Emphasis on climate change adaptation, groundwater recharge, and protection of water bodies from pollution.
• The draft policy advocates for community participation and data-driven decision-making in water governance.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers
prelimsPoints: Water is a State subject in India (Entry 17, List II, Seventh Schedule).
The Ministry of Jal Shakti was formed by merging the Ministry of Water Resources, River Development & Ganga Rejuvenation and Ministry of Drinking Water and Sanitation.
Jal Jeevan Mission aims to provide safe and adequate drinking water through tap connections to all rural households by 2024.
India is one of the most water-stressed countries globally.
mainsPoints: Analyze the critical challenges in water resource management in India and how the Draft National Water Policy 2025 seeks to address them.
Discuss the concept of integrated river basin management and its potential to resolve inter-state water disputes and promote sustainable water use.
Evaluate the measures proposed in the policy to enhance water use efficiency in agriculture and industry, considering India's water scarcity.
Examine the role of community participation, data-driven governance, and climate change adaptation in achieving water security for India.
GS3.imageDescription: A flowing river with agricultural fields on its banks, symbolizing integrated water management.
GS3.trivia: The first National Water Policy in India was adopted in 1987, followed by revisions in 2002 and 2012.

GS3.headline: Report Highlights Progress in India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) Adoption
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by a leading think tank highlighted India's rapid progress in the adoption and scaling of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The report noted significant achievements in financial inclusion through UPI, identity verification via Aadhaar, and data sharing with DigiLocker.
• India's DPI model is being increasingly recognized globally as a blueprint for digital transformation in developing countries.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access, digital literacy, and robust cybersecurity frameworks.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to further expand DPI services and ensure inclusive digital growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: DPI refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI.
DigiLocker is a cloud-based platform for issuance and verification of documents.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative impact of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) on financial inclusion, governance, and economic development.
Discuss the key components of India's DPI stack and how they contribute to a robust digital ecosystem.
Examine the challenges associated with scaling DPI, including ensuring digital literacy, equitable access, and data privacy.
Evaluate India's potential to export its DPI model to other developing countries and its implications for global digital cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, forming a network.
GS3.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS3.headline: Draft National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 Released for Public Feedback
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) released the Draft National Cyber Security Strategy 2025.
• The strategy aims to create a secure and resilient cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government.
• Key pillars include strengthening critical information infrastructure protection, developing indigenous cyber capabilities, and fostering international cooperation.
• Emphasis on robust regulatory frameworks, skill development in cybersecurity, and public awareness campaigns.
• The draft seeks to address evolving cyber threats and position India as a leader in cyber defense.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
prelimsPoints: CERT-In is the national agency for responding to computer security incidents.
Critical Information Infrastructure refers to facilities, systems, and assets whose incapacitation or destruction would have a debilitating impact on national security, economy, and public health.
India's first National Cyber Security Policy was launched in 2013.
The Personal Data Protection Bill (now Digital Personal Data Protection Act) addresses data privacy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the increasing cyber threats faced by India and the necessity of a comprehensive National Cyber Security Strategy.
Discuss the key objectives and proposed measures of the Draft National Cyber Security Strategy 2025 in protecting critical infrastructure and promoting a secure cyberspace.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps, international cooperation, and regulatory enforcement.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and individual awareness in enhancing national cyber resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, symbolizing national cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: The WannaCry ransomware attack in 2017 affected over 200,000 computers in 150 countries, including India.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Vaccine Breakthrough for Emerging Infectious Diseases Announced
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists announced a significant breakthrough in developing an indigenous vaccine candidate for a novel emerging infectious disease.
• The vaccine, developed using mRNA technology, showed promising results in preclinical trials.
• This achievement underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.
• The development is part of India's broader strategy to enhance self-reliance in pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors.
• Further clinical trials are expected to commence soon, with hopes for rapid deployment if successful.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: mRNA vaccines use messenger RNA to instruct cells to make a protein, triggering an immune response.
Preclinical trials are conducted in laboratories and on animals.
India is a major global producer of vaccines.
The Department of Biotechnology (DBT) promotes research in biotechnology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development, particularly using advanced technologies like mRNA, for India's public health and global health security.
Analyze how such breakthroughs contribute to India's 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision in the pharmaceutical and biotechnology sectors.
Examine the challenges in accelerating vaccine research, development, and deployment for emerging infectious diseases.
Evaluate India's role as a global pharmaceutical hub and its potential to address future pandemic threats through scientific innovation.
GS3.imageDescription: A microscopic view of a virus with a vaccine syringe in the foreground, symbolizing medical breakthrough.
GS3.trivia: India's 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative supplied COVID-19 vaccines to numerous countries during the pandemic.

GS3.headline: Progress Report on National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) Highlights Key Milestones
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released a progress report on the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP), showcasing key achievements and future targets.
• The report highlighted significant investment in sectors like roads, railways, urban infrastructure, and renewable energy.
• NIP aims to boost India's infrastructure over a five-year period (FY20-25) with an estimated outlay of ₹111 lakh crore.
• Challenges identified include land acquisition, project financing, and inter-ministerial coordination.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to accelerate project execution and attract private investment to meet NIP objectives.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: NIP was launched in 2019 with projects across various sectors.
Gati Shakti Master Plan aims for integrated planning and coordinated implementation of infrastructure connectivity projects.
Infrastructure development is crucial for sustained economic growth.
Sources of funding for NIP include central & state budgets, public sector undertakings, and private sector investment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) in driving India's economic growth and enhancing competitiveness.
Discuss the progress made under NIP across various sectors and the challenges encountered in project implementation.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships and innovative financing mechanisms in achieving NIP's ambitious targets.
Evaluate the synergy between NIP and other government initiatives like Gati Shakti and National Monetisation Pipeline in accelerating infrastructure development.
GS3.imageDescription: A montage of modern infrastructure projects like highways, bridges, and power plants.
GS3.trivia: India has the second-largest road network in the world, spanning over 6.3 million kilometers.
